title: Method and apparatus for precise measurement and dispensing of liquids
abstract: A dispenser device for dispensing fluids is described. The dispenser can include a reservoir cap that can directly attach to and replace the screw top cap of traditional screw top bottle. The reservoir cap includes an air reservoir that can facilitate movement of the fluid through the device. The reservoir cap is coupled to a dispenser that is configured to receive the fluid from the cap. The dispenser can be detached from the cap and used to directly dispense the fluid.
claims: Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A device comprising: a cap comprising: a first port configured to be coupled to a container holding a fluid therein and receive the fluid from the container; a second port; and an air reservoir configured to facilitate movement of the fluid through the cap; and a detachable dispenser coupled to the second port of the cap and configured to receive the fluid from the cap. 2. The device of Claim 1 , wherein the detachable dispenser comprises rounded comers. 3. The device of Claim 1, wherein the detachable dispenser comprises a spoon-like configuration. 4. The device of Claim 1, further comprising an adjustable volume control component coupled to the detachable dispenser. 5. The device of Claim 4, wherein the adjustable volume control component is an adjustable screw configured to adjust volume of the fluid received from the cap by the detachable dispenser. 6. The device of Claim 4, wherein the adjustable volume control component is configured to adjust volume of the fluid to a predetermined volume. 7. The device of Claim 4, wherein the adjustable volume control component includes one or more markings for adjusting the volume of the fluid to one or more specific volumes. 8. The device of Claim 7, wherein the one or more markings are etched into the adjustable volume control component. 9. The device of Claim 4, wherein the adjustable volume control component comprises a seal configured to prevent leakage. 10. The device of Claim 1, wherein die container is a screw top container. 11. The device of Claim 10, wherein the first port is configured to be coupled to threads of the screw top container to close the container. 12. The device of Claim 1, wherein the detachable dispenser comprises a barrel disposed at an angle less than 90 degrees with respect to normal. 13. The device of Claim 1 , wherein the cap further comprises a display configured to alert a user of at least one of amount of the fluid to dispense using the device and time for dispensing the fluid using the device. 14. The device of Claim 1, further comprising at least one of a visual or an audio indicator configured to alert a user to use the device at predetermined time intervals. 15. The device of Claim 14, wherein the visual indicator is an LED lamp configured to turn on or off at the predet ermined time intervals. 16. The device of Claim 1, wherein the cap and the detachable dispenser comprise complementary features configured to couple the detachable dispenser to the second port of the cap. 17. The device of Claim 1, wherein the detachable dispenser comprises one or more features configured to catch spills and leaks of the fluid received from the cap. 18. The device of Claim 1, further comprising a processor configured to control operation of the device. 19. The device of Claim 18, wherein processor is configured to control at least one of amount of the fluid to dispense using the device and time for dispensing the fluid using the device. 20. The device of Claim 1, further comprising a communications interface configured to connect the device to at least one of a nearby communications interface of another device of a communications network. 21. The device of Claim 20, wherein the communications interface is a Bluetooth chip. 22. The device of Claim 1, further comprising a timer configured to monitor a time at which the fluid is dispensed. 23. The device of Claim 22, wherein the timer is configured to alert a user at predetermined time interval to dispense the fluid using the device. 24. The device of Claim 22, wherein the timer is configured to activate to monitor the time upon attachment of the cap to the detachable dispenser. 25. The device of Claim 1, wherein the cap further comprises a sensor configured to monitor at least one of amount of the fluid to dispense using the device and time for dispensing the fluid using the device. 26. The device of Claim 25, wherein the sensor is at least one of an infrared sensor, an ultrasonic sensor, a mechanical switch, and an electrical switch. 27. The device of Claim 1 , wherein the fluid is a viscous fluid. 28. A container cap for closing a container holding a fluid therein, the container cap comprising: a first port configured to be coupled to the container and receive the fluid from the container; an air reservoir configured to facilitate movement of the fluid through the container cap; and a second port comprising at least one valve configured to close the container cap and prevent movement of fluid therefrom. 29. The container cap of Claim 28, wherein the second port is configured to connect to a detachable dispenser. 30. The container cap of Claim 29, wherein the at least one valve is configured to open to allow movement of the fluid out of the cap and into the detachable dispenser. 31. The container cap of Claim 29, wherein the cap and the detachable dispenser comprise complementary features configured to couple the detachable dispenser to the second port of the cap.
